items.body:
## đźŽŻ Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This is a confirmed bug in the management plane SDK generator (`Azure.Generator.Management`) where `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` calls `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last()`, which throws `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` for multi-service combined clients (like Compute). The root cause is a flawed assumption that a single global API version exists for all clients, which doesn't hold for specs using the `@client({ service: [...] })` combined-client pattern.

**Reporter:** Repository member â€” not customer-reported.
**Blocking:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` TypeSpec migration.
**Affected component:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs` (line 147) and its 3 callers.

<details>
<summary>đź“‹ Root Cause Analysis</summary>

The bug is in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs`:

```csharp
// Line 147 â€” crashes when ApiVersions is empty (multi-service combined clients)
internal string DefaultApiVersion => InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last();
```

The Compute TypeSpec spec uses a combined-client pattern:

```typespec
`@client`({
  name: "ComputeClient",
  service: [Compute, ComputeDisk, ComputeGallery, ComputeSku],
})
namespace ComputeCombine;
```

In this pattern:
- The top-level `InputNamespace.ApiVersions` is **empty** (the aggregated namespace has no direct API versions).
- API versions live **per sub-service**, surfaced through operation parameter defaults:
  - `Compute` â†’ `2025-04-01`, `ComputeDisk` â†’ `2025-01-02`, `ComputeGallery` â†’ `2025-03-03`, `ComputeSku` â†’ `2021-07-01`

</details>

<details>
<summary>đź”§ Suggested Fix Approach</summary>

The `DefaultApiVersion` property needs to be removed or made per-REST-client. There are 3 call sites to update:

| File | Line | Current Usage |
|------|------|---------------|
| `MockableResourceProvider.cs` | 116 | Passes as literal to REST client ctor |
| `ResourceClientProvider.cs` | 298 | `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))` |
| `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs` | 291 | Same pattern |

**Recommended fix:** Each of these 3 providers already knows which REST client it's generating for. They should derive the API version from the REST client's own operations (look up the `apiVersion` constant/enum parameter default on any operation belonging to that client), rather than using a global value.

A safe fallback chain:
1. Try to get API version from the specific `InputClient`'s operations' `apiVersion` parameter default
2. Fall back to `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.LastOrDefault()` for single-service specs (backward compat)
3. If still null, throw a descriptive exception

```csharp
// Example helper
private static string? GetApiVersionForClient(InputClient client)
{
    return client.Operations
        .SelectMany(op => op.Parameters)
        .FirstOrDefault(p => p.Name == "apiVersion" && p.DefaultValue != null)
        ?.DefaultValue?.Value as string;
}
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>đź§Ş Reproduction Steps</summary>

1. Clone the Compute TypeSpec spec from `azure-rest-api-specs`
2. Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/compute/resource-manager/Microsoft.Compute/Compute`
3. Add `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter to the spec's `tspconfig.yaml`
4. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`
5. Generator crashes: `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` at `ManagementInputLibrary.get_DefaultApiVersion()`

**Quick unit test repro:** Create an `InputNamespace` with empty `ApiVersions` and multiple child `InputClient` instances with `apiVersion` parameters, then invoke `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>âś… Sub-task Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Remove/refactor `DefaultApiVersion` in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs` to avoid crashing on empty `ApiVersions`
- [ ] Update `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` to derive API version from the specific REST client's operations
- [ ] Update `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` similarly
- [ ] Update `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` similarly
- [ ] Add a unit/integration test covering the multi-service combined-client scenario (empty top-level `ApiVersions`)
- [ ] Validate Compute TypeSpec generation succeeds end-to-end after the fix
- [ ] Run `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to regenerate test projects and ensure no regressions

</details>

<details>
<summary>đź“š Relevant Resources</summary>

- [ManagementInputLibrary.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs) â€” line 147
- [MockableResourceProvider.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/MockableResourceProvider.cs) â€” line 116
- [TypeSpec `@client` decorator docs]((typespec.io/redacted) â€” combined client pattern
- [http-client-csharp-mgmt generator README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)

</details>

**Owners (per CODEOWNERS):** `@JoshLove-msft` `@m-nash` `@jorgerangel-msft` `@live1206`
